items.final_wording: Supports symptom history, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, clinical context, and ambulatory ECG review for syncope or palpitations without concluding causation.

source.title: 2017 ISHNE-HRS expert consensus statement on ambulatory ECG and external cardiac monitoring/telemetry
source.population: Adults and selected paediatric patients requiring ambulatory ECG or external cardiac monitoring for symptom-rhythm correlation or defined clinical indications.
source.setting: Outpatient and ambulatory rhythm evaluation, monitor selection, recording, analysis, and clinician result review.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting symptoms, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, monitor type and duration context, recording quality, and clinician review of Holter or ambulatory ECG data. It is old and does not generate a rhythm diagnosis, monitor order, treatment, or referral.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports symptom history, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, clinical context, and ambulatory ECG review for syncope or palpitations without concluding causation.

items.rationale: The ISHNE-HRS section supports documenting the indication, symptom history and frequency, event diary context, symptom-rhythm correlation, and clinician review of ambulatory ECG data without inferring causation or a rhythm diagnosis.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting monitor type and duration in relation to symptom frequency and recording window; treatment and diagnosis are outside scope.

source.title: 2017 ISHNE-HRS expert consensus statement on ambulatory ECG and external cardiac monitoring/telemetry
source.population: Adults and selected paediatric patients requiring ambulatory ECG or external cardiac monitoring for symptom-rhythm correlation or defined clinical indications.
source.setting: Outpatient and ambulatory rhythm evaluation, monitor selection, recording, analysis, and clinician result review.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting symptoms, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, monitor type and duration context, recording quality, and clinician review of Holter or ambulatory ECG data. It is old and does not generate a rhythm diagnosis, monitor order, treatment, or referral.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations table - selection of ambulatory ECG

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting monitor type and duration in relation to symptom frequency and recording window; treatment and diagnosis are outside scope.

items.rationale: The recommendations table supports documenting monitor type, recording duration, and whether the recording window matched symptom frequency; it does not generate a new monitor order.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician selection and documentation of monitoring technology based on symptom frequency, diagnostic power, recording duration, patient acceptance, and recording quality; it does not order a device automatically.

source.title: 2017 ISHNE-HRS expert consensus statement on ambulatory ECG and external cardiac monitoring/telemetry
source.population: Adults and selected paediatric patients requiring ambulatory ECG or external cardiac monitoring for symptom-rhythm correlation or defined clinical indications.
source.setting: Outpatient and ambulatory rhythm evaluation, monitor selection, recording, analysis, and clinician result review.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting symptoms, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, monitor type and duration context, recording quality, and clinician review of Holter or ambulatory ECG data. It is old and does not generate a rhythm diagnosis, monitor order, treatment, or referral.

exact_location.heading: Section 3.1.6 - selection of appropriate ambulatory ECG technology

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician selection and documentation of monitoring technology based on symptom frequency, diagnostic power, recording duration, patient acceptance, and recording quality; it does not order a device automatically.

items.rationale: Technology selection and recording-quality considerations were reviewed; the generic legacy scaffold had no specific device-quality item to map.
